violations.claim: PROVEN: else if structure prevents both then-branch and else-branch from firing
violations.smt2:
; PROVEN: else if structure prevents both then-branch and else-branch from firing
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 246
(declare-const in_consequence Int)   ; 1 = isDescendantOf(logNode, consequence) = true
(declare-const in_alternative Int)   ; 1 = isDescendantOf(logNode, alternative) = true
(declare-const pos_added Int)        ; 1 = condition.text added (then-branch path)
(declare-const neg_added Int)        ; 1 = !(condition.text) added (else-branch path)

; Tree-sitter structural invariant: consequence and alternative are disjoint subtrees
(assert (=> (= in_consequence 1) (= in_alternative 0)))
(assert (=> (= in_alternative 1) (= in_consequence 0)))

; Code transition (else if): then-branch fires exclusively
(assert (=> (= in_consequence 1) (= pos_added 1)))
(assert (=> (= in_consequence 1) (= neg_added 0)))

; Code transition (else if): else-branch fires exclusively
(assert (=> (= in_alternative 1) (= neg_added 1)))
(assert (=> (= in_alternative 1) (= pos_added 0)))

; Negation: both added in a single iteration
(assert (= pos_added 1))
(assert (= neg_added 1))
(check-sat)
